fixed_blocks.text:
18 or patented. In application, a Professor required to have a mandatory research WL of 6 must have at least 2 research studies.

Section 32 On extension, short-term training/seminar shall be given 1-unit equivalent WL while long-term extension projects shall have an equivalent WL of 3 units. A short-term extension activity ranges from 6 months and below. A long-term extension activity is from seven (7) months and above. In case of no workload release, the extensionist(s) shall received cash incentive(s) subject to usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations shall be divided.

Section 33 Special courses offered upon request of students and agreed to be handled by a faculty even without remuneration must not be included as a WL/TL.

Section 34 In case of budget constraints, the teaching personnel who render services in excess of the regular teaching load or outside of the regular office hours may be paid in a form of non-monetary remuneration pursuant to existing Civil Service rules and regulations, provided that he/she is informed and has given prior consent and approval as to the mode of compensation. The Human Resource Management Office shall facilitate the preparation of the Affidavit of Undertaking.

Section 35 Faculty members without designations handling courses in specific programs, as provided in their respective programs, standards, and guidelines (PSG), shall be allowed not more than thirty-six (36) teaching hours per week and shall receive corresponding remuneration for the teaching overload.

35.1 Full-time faculty members may carry a combined RLE and classroom load of not more than thirty-six (36) hours per week which include consultation hours and other activities related to RLE instruction, research and extension (CMO 15, s.2017).

35.2 The Dean and the Department Chair/Head may be allowed to handle a maximum teaching load of not more than 12 hours a week provided that their primary duties and responsibilities are not neglected.

qa_pairs.context:
18 or patented. In application, a Professor required to have a mandatory research WL of 6 must have at least 2 research studies.

Section 32 On extension, short-term training/seminar shall be given 1-unit equivalent WL while long-term extension projects shall have an equivalent WL of 3 units. A short-term extension activity ranges from 6 months and below. A long-term extension activity is from seven (7) months and above. In case of no workload release, the extensionist(s) shall received cash incentive(s) subject to usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations shall be divided.

Section 33 Special courses offered upon request of students and agreed to be handled by a faculty even without remuneration must not be included as a WL/TL.

Section 34 In case of budget constraints, the teaching personnel who render services in excess of the regular teaching load or outside of the regular office hours may be paid in a form of non-monetary remuneration pursuant to existing Civil Service rules and regulations, provided that he/she is informed and has given prior consent and approval as to the mode of compensation. The Human Resource Management Office shall facilitate the preparation of the Affidavit of Undertaking.

Section 35 Faculty members without designations handling courses in specific programs, as provided in their respective programs, standards, and guidelines (PSG), shall be allowed not more than thirty-six (36) teaching hours per week and shall receive corresponding remuneration for the teaching overload.

35.1 Full-time faculty members may carry a combined RLE and classroom load of not more than thirty-six (36) hours per week which include consultation hours and other activities related to RLE instruction, research and extension (CMO 15, s.2017).

35.2 The Dean and the Department Chair/Head may be allowed to handle a maximum teaching load of not more than 12 hours a week provided that their primary duties and responsibilities are not neglected.
qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum teaching load that a full-time faculty member can carry?
qa_pairs.answer: Not more than 36 hours per week, including consultation hours and other activities related to RLE instruction, research, and extension.

A short-term extension activity ranges from 6 months and below. A long-term extension activity is from seven (7) months and above. In case of no workload release, the extensionist(s) shall received cash incentive(s) subject to usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations shall be divided.

Section 35 Faculty members without designations handling courses in specific programs, as provided in their respective programs, standards, and guidelines (PSG), shall be allowed not more than thirty-six (36) teaching hours per week and shall receive corresponding remuneration for the teaching overload.

35.1 Full-time faculty members may carry a combined RLE and classroom load of not more than thirty-six (36) hours per week which include consultation hours and other activities related to RLE instruction, research and extension (CMO 15, s.2017).

35.2 The Dean and the Department Chair/Head may be allowed to handle a maximum teaching load of not more than 12 hours a week provided that their primary duties and responsibilities are not neglected.
qa_pairs.question: Can a Dean or Department Chair/Head handle a maximum teaching load of more than 12 hours per week?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the Dean and Department Chair/Head may be allowed to handle a maximum teaching load of not more than 12 hours per week, provided that their primary duties and responsibilities are not neglected.

qa_pairs.answer: Short-term extension projects are those that range from 6 months and below.

qa_pairs.answer: Non-monetary remuneration refers to forms of compensation other than cash, such as recognition or appreciation for work done.

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of RLE and classroom load?
qa_pairs.answer: RLE stands for Research, Extension, and Leadership, while classroom load refers to the number of hours a faculty member spends teaching courses in a regular semester.

qa_pairs.question: Can a faculty member carry a combined RLE and classroom load of more than 36 hours per week?
qa_pairs.answer: Full-time faculty members may carry a combined RLE and classroom load of not more than thirty-six (36) hours per week, which include consultation hours and other activities related to RLE instruction, research, and extension.

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of budget constraints?
qa_pairs.answer: Budget constraints refer to situations where the institution or department does not have sufficient funds to cover all teaching loads.

qa_pairs.question: Can a faculty member handle a teaching load that exceeds their designated load?
qa_pairs.answer: Faculty members without designations handling courses in specific programs shall be allowed not more than thirty-six (36) teaching hours per week and shall receive corresponding remuneration for the teaching overload.

qa_pairs.context:
18 or patented. In application, a Professor required to have a mandatory research WL of 6 must have at least 2 research studies.

Section 32 On extension, short-term training/seminar shall be given 1-unit equivalent WL while long-term extension projects shall have an equivalent WL of 3 units. A short-term extension activity ranges from 6 months and below. A long-term extension activity is from seven (7) months and above. In case of no workload release, the extensionist(s) shall received cash incentive(s) subject to usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations shall be divided.

Section 33 Special courses offered upon request of students and agreed to be handled by a faculty even without remuneration must not be included as a WL/TL.

Section 34 In case of budget constraints, the teaching personnel who render services in excess of the regular teaching load or outside of the regular office hours may be paid in a form of non-monetary remuneration pursuant to existing Civil Service rules and regulations, provided that he/she is informed and has given prior consent and approval as to the mode of compensation. The Human Resource Management Office shall facilitate the preparation of the Affidavit of Undertaking.

Section 35 Faculty members without designations handling courses in specific programs, as provided in their respective programs, standards, and guidelines (PSG), shall be allowed not more than thirty-six (36) teaching hours per week and shall receive corresponding remuneration for the teaching overload.

35.1 Full-time faculty members may carry a combined RLE and classroom load of not more than thirty-six (36) hours per week which include consultation hours and other activities related to RLE instruction, research and extension (CMO 15, s.2017).

35.2 The Dean and the Department Chair/Head may be allowed to handle a maximum teaching load of not more than 12 hours a week provided that their primary duties and responsibilities are not neglected.
qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of consultation hours?
qa_pairs.answer: Consultation hours refer to the time a faculty member spends with students outside of regular class hours, such as during office hours or by appointment.

qa_pairs.question: How are cash incentives divided among extensionists in case of budget constraints?
qa_pairs.answer: The cash incentives shall be divided according to usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of a faculty member without designation?
qa_pairs.answer: A faculty member without designation refers to a teacher who does not have a specific academic rank or title, such as Professor, Associate Professor, or Assistant Professor.

qa_pairs.question: Can a Dean or Department Chair/Head handle a teaching load that exceeds their designated load?
qa_pairs.answer: The Dean and Department Chair/Head may be allowed to handle a maximum teaching load of not more than 12 hours per week, provided that their primary duties and responsibilities are not neglected.

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of remuneration?
qa_pairs.answer: Remuneration refers to forms of compensation received in exchange for work or services performed, such as salary, wages, bonuses, or benefits.
